name: Inference
oneliner: Running a trained model to produce outputs — the phase where latency, throughput, and memory bandwidth become the dominant constraints.
explanation: Inference is the process of feeding input into a trained model and reading its output. For autoregressive LLMs, inference has two distinct phases. Prefill processes the entire input prompt in parallel, building the KV cache — this phase is compute-bound. Decode then generates tokens one at a time, each step reading the full KV cache but computing only one new token — this phase is memory-bandwidth-bound because most time is spent loading weights from GPU memory rather than doing arithmetic. This is why KV-cache quantization, speculative decoding, and batching are critical for efficient serving.
fundamentals: During prefill, cost $\approx 2nP$ FLOPs where $n$ = input tokens, $P$ = parameters. During decode, each step costs $\approx 2P$ FLOPs but must load all $P$ parameters from HBM. Arithmetic intensity during decode is $\approx 1/\text{bytes\_per\_param}$, well below GPU compute-to-bandwidth ratio, making decode firmly memory-bandwidth-bound. Batching $B$ requests raises intensity to $B/\text{bytes\_per\_param}$.

foundational_papers.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
